steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "When you place your ______ the pitch will shift as your hands move through the air." The blank fills the noun phrase "hands between the two antennas" plus the comma that separates the introductory adverbial clause from the main clause. Both nouns are simple plurals (more than one hand, more than one antenna) — no possession is involved, so no apostrophe is needed on either word.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice using the apostrophe and possessive rule
steps.content:
• Choice A — hand's between the two antenna's,: Apostrophe-s on both nouns signals possession (the hand owns something; the antennas own something), but nothing is owned here. Violates the rule that apostrophes mark possession, not plurality. ✗
  • Choice B — hands between the two antennas,: Plain plurals with no apostrophes, and the comma correctly closes the introductory clause before the main clause begins. No rule is violated. ✓
  • Choice C — hands' between the two antennas',: "hands'" and "antennas'" both incorrectly mark possession where none exists; the extra comma after "antennas'" also creates faulty punctuation. Violates the rule against using apostrophes for plurals. ✗
  • Choice D — hands' between the two antennas,: "hands'" incorrectly marks possession on the plural noun; only the plain plural "hands" is needed here. Violates the rule against using apostrophes for plurals. ✗
